skill.name: senior-prompt-engineer
skill.description: Prompt engineering skill for designing, testing, and improving production LLM prompts, RAG workflows, structured outputs, and agent patterns.
skill.summary: Optimize LLM prompts and evaluation workflows for production AI systems.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the weak-cryptography and reconnaissance matches as false positives caused by documentation terms, argparse descriptions, and prior evaluation text. The skill includes Python CLI stubs and markdown command examples, but I found no automatic command execution, network access, credential access, cryptographic implementation, exfiltration, or prompt-injection language. Final risk is low because users should still review documented shell commands before manual use.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto And Reconnaissance Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are prose or harmless parser descriptions, not cryptographic code or reconnaissance behavior. Examples include design text, resource awareness language, and argparse descriptions. No network scanning, weak crypto implementation, or exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain markdown prose, argparse setup, or previous audit text. I found no crypto APIs, scanning logic, network calls, or data exfiltration patterns.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Require Manual Review
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains fenced markdown command examples for Python, Docker, kubectl, Helm, and related tools. They are documentation examples and are not executed by the skill, but users should review commands before running them manually.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is clearly inside markdown code fences, so automatic execution risk is low. Manual command misuse remains a minor operational risk.

content.value_statement: Production LLM prompts can fail when requirements, examples, and evaluation checks are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design prompts, RAG workflows, and agent patterns with structured review steps.
actual_capabilities: Guides prompt design for LLM tasks, structured outputs, and agent workflows.
Provides reference material for prompt patterns, LLM evaluation, and agentic system design.
Includes Python CLI stubs for prompt optimization, RAG evaluation, and agent orchestration workflows.
Supports planning for reliability, observability, testing, and production deployment practices.
Helps define evaluation criteria for accuracy, consistency, latency, and edge cases.
limitations: The included Python scripts are stubs and do not implement full optimization logic.
It does not call external LLM APIs or provide model credentials.
It does not replace human review for safety, policy, or product requirements.
Some documentation is generic and should be adapted to each project.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Prompts
use_cases.description: Design prompts with clear roles, constraints, examples, and measurable evaluation checks.

use_cases.title: Plan LLM Evaluation
use_cases.description: Create repeatable review criteria for prompt quality, RAG behavior, and regression testing.

use_cases.title: Shape AI Feature Requirements
use_cases.description: Translate product goals into prompt behaviors, user flows, limits, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prompt Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for clarity. Identify vague instructions, missing context, and better wording. Keep the improved prompt simple and testable.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Output Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a prompt that returns a consistent structured answer. Define required fields, validation rules, refusal behavior, and one short example.

prompt_templates.title: RAG Answer Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this RAG prompt. Check citation rules, missing-context handling, hallucination risks, and test cases for conflicting source material.

prompt_templates.title: Agent Workflow Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent workflow for tool boundaries, state handling, recovery paths, safety checks, and measurable success criteria.

output_examples.input: Improve a support chatbot prompt for billing questions.
output: Clarified the assistant role and allowed billing topics.
Added escalation rules for refunds, disputes, and account ownership issues.
Defined response checks for accuracy, tone, and missing account context.

output_examples.input: Create an evaluation plan for a RAG assistant.
output: Listed test cases for answer accuracy, citation quality, and missing evidence handling.
Added failure categories for unsupported claims and stale source material.
Recommended regression checks before each prompt change reaches production.

output_examples.input: Review an agent prompt that uses tools.
output: Separated planning, tool use, and final answer responsibilities.
Added rules for tool errors, user confirmation, and irreversible actions.
Defined measurable success criteria for safe task completion.

best_practices: Start with task goals, input assumptions, output format, and explicit failure behavior.
Test prompts with normal cases, edge cases, adversarial inputs, and empty context.
Version prompts with evaluation notes so regressions are easier to diagnose.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on vague style instructions without measurable acceptance criteria.
Do not place secrets, private customer data, or hidden policies in reusable prompts.
Do not ship agent prompts without tool boundaries, recovery paths, and human review points.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps design production prompts, RAG behavior, structured outputs, evaluations, and agent workflows.

faq.question: Does it run LLM API calls for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and local script stubs, but it does not call external model APIs.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt design guidance applies to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and similar coding assistants.

faq.question: Are the included scripts complete tools?
faq.answer: No. The scripts are basic command-line stubs and need project-specific implementation before production use.

faq.question: How should I evaluate improved prompts?
faq.answer: Use representative examples, edge cases, refusal tests, and regression checks tied to product requirements.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive data workflows?
faq.answer: Use it with normal data controls. Do not place secrets or private data in reusable prompt templates.
